title: Is cosmological, ontological, and eschatological uncertainty (epistemological misconceptialization) a demanded necessary feature of following/pursuing-to-intimately-know Yeshua (e.g. Yahweh, the Shekinah, God, the Spirit)?
body:
- circa eschatological surprise/shock big switch motif in Messiah's Torah (first last, last first) 
- circa impossibility of correct conceptualization because of current-age ontological limitation ( seeing dimly into a polished piece of metal 1 Corinthians 13)
- being locked in ontological ignorance of Yeshua's current Ontology and thus ignorance of our own Ontology (which is a shared/joint ontology)
- wrong expectations of earliest church of who is in who is out, who the target of the mission is, and when the job is done
- wrong expectations of pre-Columbian church of who is in who is out, who the target of the mission is, and when the job is done (gospel preached in all nations)
excerpt: - circa eschatological surprise/shock big switch motif in Messiah's Torah (first last, last first)
